core_mechanism: a correlated (persistent) random walk driven by two independent exponentially-relaxing noise processes with separate characteristic relaxation times, whose velocity autocorrelation is a sum of decaying exponentials and whose time-integral (Green-Kubo) fixes an effective diffusion coefficient
brief_reason: The abstract decomposes the motion into decoupled speed and direction fluctuations each with its own characteristic time-scale, and derives a velocity autocorrelation that is a sum of exponential forms plus corrections to the diffusion coefficient, which is exactly the two-relaxation-time persistent-random-walk / Ornstein-Uhlenbeck transport structure.
